commit.message:
fix: disable Velopack auto-apply on startup and add local update source override

Without SetAutoApplyOnStartup(false), Velopack would auto-apply any
previously downloaded update on the next startup and exit the process.
Combined with the single-instance mutex introduced in 1.5.6, the newly
launched process would see the mutex still held and exit immediately,
leaving the user with no running app and no update applied.

Updates now only apply when the user explicitly clicks "Restart and Update".

Also adds D2C_UPDATE_SOURCE env var override for local update testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(index): associate concepts/entities with correct chunk via chunk_index

Previously _store_extraction always linked every concept and entity to
chunk_ids[0]. Now the extraction prompt requests chunk_index on each
concept and entity, and _store_extraction maps each to the correct
chunk_id using the same clamped-index pattern already used for
implicit_items. Also deduplicated n_chunks (defined once before the
concepts loop, reused for entities and implicit_items).

commit.message:
Fix: utöka SKATTETABELL_31 till 200 000 kr (var 120 000 kr)

Lägger till 8 nya datapunkter (125k–200k). Värdet för 135k är
verifierat mot SKV:s kalkylator (netto 82 298 kr). Värden för
125k och 130k är korrigerade från användarens uppskattning (de var
icke-monotona) via linjär interpolation med ~35,57 % marginalskatt.

Uppdaterar även testets lön från 150k (nu i tabellen) till 250k
för att fortsatt testa extrapolation utanför tabellens övre gräns.

commit.message:
Fix bullet truncation: raise write_sti_statement token budget from 512 to 4096

Gemini 2.5 Flash uses thinking tokens that share the output budget.
At 512 tokens, thinking alone can exhaust the limit before the bullet
is complete, causing mid-sentence truncation.

commit.message:
legion v2: real computer control + agentic tool loop

computer_agent.py (new):
- auto-detects X DISPLAY (env → probe :0/:1/:2 → who → fallback)
- take_screenshot() with 4 fallback methods (scrot, import, gnome-screenshot, xwd)
- full mouse: click/move/drag/scroll with xdotool
- full keyboard: type text, press shortcuts (ctrl+t, alt+Tab, etc.)
- window management: list/focus/minimize/maximize with wmctrl+xdotool
- app launcher: open 20+ apps by name (whatsapp, supabase, vscode, gmail, etc.)
- browser control: navigate, new tab, switch tabs, close tab
- file ops: read/write/append/list_directory/open_folder_gui
- clipboard: get/set via xclip/xsel
- self-upgrade: install_packages() + restart_bot() via os.execv

llm_client.py:
- agent_loop(): full agentic tool-calling loop using Groq function calling
  LLM autonomously calls tools (shell, click, screenshot, open app) until done
  sends progress updates + screenshots to Telegram in real-time
- chat(): unchanged single-turn mode
- take_screenshot(): delegates to computer_agent, auto-detects DISPLAY

main.py:
- /do <task>: agentic computer control with live step-by-step progress
- /screen: screenshot with Analyze + Do task inline buttons
- /open <app|url>: open apps, URLs, or folders
- /click <x> <y> [button]: click at coordinates
- /type <text>: keyboard input
- /key <combo>: keyboard shortcut (ctrl+t, alt+Tab, etc.)
- /install <pkgs>: pip install + auto-restart
- /upgrade: git pull + auto-restart
- smart NL routing: Indonesian keywords detected for computer tasks
- /do shows live step counter as agent works

commit.message:
feat(auth): access control redesign + DB-backed stores [#1059] (#1086)

* feat(auth): access control redesign — entity-centric defineAccess, plans, billing, versioning

Complete access control redesign across 6 phases (Phase 6 deferred):

- Phase 1: Entity-centric defineAccess() with hierarchical role inheritance, 21 validation rules
- Phase 2: Plan features, multi-limit resolution, canBatch(), add-on support, billing periods
- Phase 3: Override store with add/max modes, overage billing computation
- Phase 4: Plan versioning via SHA-256 hashing, grandfathering with grace periods, migration API
- Phase 5: BillingAdapter interface, StripeBillingAdapter, webhook handler, overage computation
- Phase 7: Client-side plan event broadcasting via WebSocket
- DB-backed auth stores: 7 store implementations with dialect-aware DDL

Closes #1072, Closes #1073, Closes #1074, Closes #1075, Closes #1076, Closes #1078

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(auth): update auth guide for DB-backed stores and access redesign [#1059]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): reduce bcrypt rounds in tests to fix MFA test timeouts [#1059]

Use VERTZ_TEST=1 env var (auto-loaded from .env.test by Bun) to reduce
bcrypt rounds from 12 to 4 in test environments. Add explicit timeouts
to slow MFA integration tests. Add global test timeout in bunfig.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): add type annotations to authModels for isolatedDeclarations [#1059]

bunup treats TS9013 as errors in CI but warnings locally. Adding
explicit ModelDef type annotations to the exported authModels object
satisfies isolatedDeclarations requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): use Record<string, ModelDef> to avoid isolatedDeclarations cascade [#1059]

Using typeof in ModelDef generics forces non-exported table variables to
also need type annotations (TS9010). Use Record<string, ModelDef> instead
since authModels is internal infrastructure spread into createDb().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: ENCRYPTION_KEY obligatoire, suppression du fallback hardcodé

Avant : si ENCRYPTION_KEY était absente, une clé hardcodée était utilisée
en debug_assertions (dangereux si le build debug est déployé en prod).

Après :
- encryption_key() retourne Result<[u8;32], String>
- Erreur claire si absente ou trop courte (< 32 octets)
- Vérification minimale de longueur (32 octets / 64 hex)
- Les erreurs se propagent via ? dans encrypt() et decrypt()

Pour le dev local : ajouter ENCRYPTION_KEY dans .env (32+ chars).
Générer une clé : openssl rand -hex 32

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use OIDC trusted publishing instead of NPM token

Remove NODE_AUTH_TOKEN and registry-url from publish workflow.
OIDC trusted publisher is already configured on npmjs.com — let
npm authenticate via the GitHub Actions OIDC token instead of a
classic/granular access token that conflicts with 2FA.

commit.message:
Refactor substitute route: pre-filter DB candidates by movement pattern

Instead of asking AI to generate exercise names (fragile, can pick wrong
category), now query the DB for valid candidates first and ask AI to select
from that list. Pipeline:

1. Map workout day type → allowed movementPatterns via ALLOWED_PATTERNS constant
   (pull→pull/hinge, push→push, legs→squat/hinge/carry, etc.)
2. Query exercises matching those patterns + overlapping primary muscles (pass 1)
   Widen to all allowed-pattern exercises if < 3 results (pass 2)
3. Strip candidates whose injuryFlags match user's active conditions (code layer)
4. AI selects + ranks from pre-validated list — exerciseIds are in the prompt
5. Hydrate response from candidateMap — no second DB round-trip

AI can no longer suggest an exercise from the wrong movement category because
wrong-category exercises are structurally absent from the candidate list.
Also reduces max_tokens (600→400) and temperature (0.7→0.5) since the task
is now selection/ranking, not open-ended generation.

commit.message:
ci: add -lws2_32 to Windows FFmpeg link flags for Tesseract

Tesseract's libtesseract.a uses Windows sockets (WSAStartup etc.)
but lists ws2_32 as a PRIVATE CMake dependency, so it doesn't appear
in tesseract.pc's Libs.private. Add -lws2_32 explicitly to the
Windows extra-ldflags to resolve the symbols at static link time.

commit.message:
fix: bypass FunctionsClient — use raw fetch in invokeFunction with explicit JWT

The Supabase FunctionsClient only calls setAuth() on SIGNED_IN/TOKEN_REFRESHED
events, not on INITIAL_SESSION (session restored from storage). This caused
invoke() to send the anon key instead of the user JWT, resulting in "Invalid JWT"
errors from the edge function.

Fix: replace supabase.functions.invoke() with a raw fetch() call. JWT is read
fresh from supabase.auth.getSession() on every invokeFunction call, guaranteeing
the correct token is always used regardless of auth event lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Strip RRULE: prefix in frequency parsing + polish routines views

Root cause: recurrence_rule values stored with "RRULE:" prefix (e.g.,
"RRULE:FREQ=MONTHLY") caused FREQ key to parse as "RRULE:FREQ",
falling through to "daily" default. Fixed in all 4 parsing locations.

Checklist: richer rows with period labels ("Due this week"), owner names,
tags, frequency-colored dots for last 7 days, and owner avatars.

Dots: now frequency-aware — weekly routines show satisfied state for
the whole week when completed on any day, not just the completion day.
Grouped by frequency with colored section headers.

Timeline: rewritten as audit trail — chronological list of completions
grouped by date with vertical timeline, frequency filters, and
relative date labels. No longer a heatmap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Steam sync stats, profil enrichi & migration consolidée

## Backend (Rust)
- Steam sync : calcul et stockage de total_achievements_count,
  games_completed, total_possible_achievements dans users après chaque sync
  (recalcul bulk + triggers incrémentaux PostgreSQL)
- user_game_stats : upsert par jeu après chaque sync Steam (complétion %)
- Nouveau module crypto.rs : chiffrement AES-256-GCM de la clé API Steam
  personnelle (steam_api_key_enc sur users)
- API /platforms/:platform/sync : retourne stats détaillées post-sync
- API /users/:username : expose completed_games (jeux 100%)
- Steam OpenID : flux complet auth + callback + enregistrement connexion

## Frontend (profile.html)
- Affichage des 3 compteurs header : Score, Succès, 100%
- Section jeux complétés à 100% : galerie scrollable avec badges verts
- Carte résultat post-sync : jeux synchro, succès débloqués, complétés
- Rang mondial → Rang

## Migrations
- Consolidation de 14 migrations en 1 fichier initial_schema.sql
  (extensions, enums, tables, triggers, index, seeds en ordre topologique)
- Déploiement production : psql --single-transaction ou sqlx auto

## Code review findings
- 🟡 N+1 sur lookup achievements Steam (à optimiser)
- 🟡 Pas de rate limiting Steam API
- 🟢 AES-256-GCM correct, pas d'injection SQL, triggers DB cohérents

commit.message:
Add images to universe portal cards

- Portal I (Cast): Lower image to fix forehead cutoff
- Portal II (Bloodline): Add kingdavid-backzoom visual header
- Portal III (Rise): Add portrait-side-battle visual header
- Portal IV (Archives): Add davidwithsword as full card background
- Convert portrait-side-battle.jpg and davidwithsword.jpeg to WebP
- Add responsive styles for portal visuals

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add CONFIG-CHANGES.md — GitOps pattern for Nextcloud config

Explains the shift from occ/direct config.php edits to Helm values.
Critical context for team discussion on stateless tenant rollout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(onchain-payments): migrate to PaymentOrchestrator — close direct execution carve-out

Route main payment and best-effort fee dispatch through
payment_orchestrator.execute_chain() instead of calling
chain_executor.dispatch_payment() directly. Removes the last
direct-execution carve-out identified in TDD remediation; adds
payment_orchestrator field to OnChainPaymentDependencies and maps
PolicyViolationError/ComplianceViolationError to 403, ChainExecutionError
to 502.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
